where the downloaded content is stored\n    \"\"\"\n    if not link:\n        return False\n\n    if \"download\" not in link:\n        command = [\"curl\", \"--output\", \"/tmp/.tmp_download_link\", link.rstrip(\"\\n\")]\n        try:\n            subprocess.run(command, capture_output=True, text=True, check=True)\n        except Exception as e:\n            logging.warning(f\"Unable to download from link: {link} due to {e}\")\n            return False\n\n        pattern = r'https?://[^\\s]*\\bdownload\\b[^\\s]*(?=\")'\n\n        download_link: str | None = None\n        with open(\"/tmp/.tmp_download_link\", \"r\") as f:\n            content = f.read()\n            matches = re.findall(pattern, content, re.MULTILINE)\n            if matches:\n               download_link = matches[0]\n\n        if download_link is None:\n            logging.warning(f\"Did not find proper download link\")\n            return False\n    else:\n        download_link = link.rstrip(\"\\n\")\n\n    try:\n        logging.info(download_link)\n        subprocess.run([f\"curl {download_link} --output {output_file_path}\"], shell=True)\n    except Exception as e:\n        logging.warning(f\"Download from link: {download_link} not successful\")\n        return False\n\n    logging.info(f\"Successfully downloaded from link {download_link}\")\n    return True\n\n\ndef check_and_download_file(file_name, dir_name, output_file_path):\n    \"\"\"\n    Check if a file exists in an ownCloud directory using WebDAV and download it if found.\n\n    Parameters:\n        file_name (str): The name of the file to check and download.\n        dir_name (str): The directory path on NextCloud.\n        output_file_path (str): Path to save the downloaded file locally.\n\n    Returns:\n        bool: True if the file was found and downloaded successfully, False otherwise.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Construct WebDAV URL for the directory\n    WEBDAV_BASE_URL = f\"{OWNCLOUD_URL}/remote.php/webdav/\"\n    server_url = f\"{WEBDAV_BASE_URL}{dir_name.strip('/')}/\"\n    headers = {'Depth': '1'}  # Request depth for PROPFIND\n\n    try:\n        # Check if the file exists in the directory\n        response = requests.request(\n            method=\"PROPFIND\",\n            url=server_url,\n            headers=headers,\n            auth=HTTPBasicAuth(OWNCLOUD_USERNAME, OWNCLOUD_PASSWORD)\n        )\n\n        if response.status_code == 207:\n            # Parse the XML response to find the file\n            root = ET.fromstring(response.text)\n            for response_element in root.findall(\".//{DAV:}response\"):\n                href = response_element.find(\"{DAV:}href\").text\n                if file_name in href:\n                    logging.info(f\"File '{file_name}' found. Proceeding to download.\")\n                    \n                    # Construct full file URL\n                    file_url = server_url + file_name\n\n                    # Download the file\n                    download_response = requests.get(\n                        file_url,\n                        auth=HTTPBasicAuth(OWNCLOUD_USERNAME, OWNCLOUD_PASSWORD),\n                        stream=True\n                    )\n\n                    if download_response.status_code == 200:\n                        with open(output_file_path, \"wb\") as file:\n                            for chunk in download_response.iter_content(chunk_size=8192):\n                                file.write(chunk)\n                        logging.info(f\"File '{file_name}' downloaded successfully to '{output_file_path}'.\")\n                        return True\n                    else:\n                        logging.error(f\"Failed to download file '{file_name}'. HTTP Status: {download_response.status_code}\")\n                        return False\n\n            # File not found in the directory\n            logging.warning(f\"File '{file_name}' not found in directory '{dir_name}'.\")\n            return False\n\n        else:\n            # Log unexpected HTTP status codes\n            logging.error(f\"Error checking directory. HTTP Status: {response.status_code}, Response: {response.text}\")\n            return False\n\n    except requests.RequestException as e:\n        logging.warning(f\"Failed to check or download file in ownCloud directory: {e}\")\n        return False\n\n\ndef check_file_in_owncloud_directory(file_name, dir_name):\n    \"\"\"\n    Check if a file exists in an ownCloud directory using WebDAV\n    \"\"\"\n    server_url = f\"{OWNCLOUD_URL}/remote.php/webdav/{dir_name}\"\n    headers = {\n        'Depth': '1',\n    }\n\n    try:\n        response = requests.request(\n            method=\"PROPFIND\",\n        url=server_url,\n        headers=headers,\n        auth=HTTPBasicAuth(OWNCLOUD_USERNAME, OWNCLOUD_PASSWORD)\n        )\n    except requests.RequestException as e:\n response_element in root.findall(\".//{DAV:}response\"):\n            href = response_element.find(\"{DAV:}href\").text\n            if file_name in href:\n                logging.info(f\"File link.rstrip(\"\\n\")\n\n    try:\n        logging.info(download_link)\n        subprocess.run([f\"curl {download_link} --output {output_file_path}\"], shell=True)\n    except Exception as e:\n        logging.warning(f\"Download from link: {download_link} not successful\")\n        return False\n\n    logging.info(f\"Successfully downloaded from link {download_link}\")\n    return True\n\n\ndef check_and_download_file(file_name, dir_name, output_file_path):\n    \"\"\"\n    Check if a file exists in an ownCloud directory using WebDAV and download it if found.\n\n    Parameters:\n        file_name (str): The name of the file to check and download.\n        dir_name (str): The directory path on NextCloud.\n        output_file_path (str): Path to save the downloaded file locally.\n\n    Returns:\n        bool: True if the file was found and downloaded successfully, False otherwise.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Construct WebDAV URL for the directory\n    WEBDAV_BASE_URL = f\"{OWNCLOUD_URL}/remote.php/webdav/\"\n    server_url = f\"{WEBDAV_BASE_URL}{dir_name.strip('/')}/\"\n    headers = {'Depth': '1'}  # Request depth for PROPFIND\n\n    try:\n        # Check if the file exists in the directory\n        response = requests.request(\n            method=\"PROPFIND\",\n            url=server_url,\n            headers=headers,\n            auth=HTTPBasicAuth(OWNCLOUD_USERNAME, OWNCLOUD_PASSWORD)\n        )\n\n        if response.status_code == 207:\n            # Parse the XML response to find the file\n            root = ET.fromstring(response.text)\n            for response_element in root.findall(\".//{DAV:}response\"):\n                href = response_element.find(\"{DAV:}href\").text\n                if file_name in href:\n                    logging.info(f\"File '{file_name}' found. Proceeding to download.\")\n                    \n                    # Construct full file URL\n                    file_url = server_url + file_name\n\n                    # Download the file\n                    download_response = requests.get(\n                        file_url,\n                        auth=HTTPBasicAuth(OWNCLOUD_USERNAME, OWNCLOUD_PASSWORD),\n                        stream=True\n                    )\n\n                    if download_response.status_code == 200:\n                        with open(output_file_path, \"wb\") as file:\n                            for chunk in download_response.iter_content(chunk_size=8192):\n                                file.write(chunk)\n                        logging.info(f\"File '{file_name}' downloaded successfully to '{output_file_path}'.\")\n                        return True\n                    else:\n                        logging.error(f\"Failed to download file '{file_name}'. HTTP Status: {download_response.status_code}\")\n                        return False\n\n            # File not found in the directory\n            logging.warning(f\"File '{file_name}' not found in directory '{dir_name}'.\")\n            return False\n\n        else:\n            # Log unexpected HTTP status codes\n            logging.error(f\"Error checking directory. HTTP Status: {response.status_code}, Response: {response.text}\")\n            return False\n\n    except requests.RequestException as e:\n        logging.warning(f\"Failed to check or download file in ownCloud directory: {e}\")\n        return False\n\n\ndef check_file_in_owncloud_directory(file_name, dir_name):\n    \"\"\"\n    Check if a file exists in an ownCloud directory using WebDAV\n BUSTUB_INT64_MIN) {\n225:         throw Exception(ExceptionType::OUT_OF_RANGE, \"Numeric value out of range.\");\n226:       }\n227:       return {type_id, bigint};\n228:     }\n229:     case Exception(ExceptionType::OUT_OF_RANGE, \"Numeric value out of range.\");\n236:       }\n237:       if (res > BUSTUB_DECIMAL_MAX || res < BUSTUB_DECIMAL_MIN) {\n238:         throw value.Copy();\n244:     default:\n245:       break;\n246:   }\n247:   throw Exception(\"VARCHAR is not coercable to \" + TypeIdToString(type_id));\n248: }\n249: }  // namespace bustub\n250: For m=2 and ρ=1, I get E ≈ 2.117, which floors to 2—that checks out. But scaling up to n_bits=14 with m=16384 and a single register at ρ=1 gives E ≈ 13006, which is way larger than expected.\n\nTesting different ρ values doesn't help much—whether ρ=2, ρ→∞, or even ρ=0, the estimate stays locked around 13006, nowhere near the target of 13009. Even pushing ρ negative doesn't shift things enough.\n\nI think I'm missing something fundamental about how the algorithm actually works. For the suffix y after removing the index bits, I need to count how many leading zeros it has before the first 1 bit appears. {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01TMDDD1HfhBVkdreLbVQMEa","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_016n88SXyWHQzwuhSgUEe5Xr","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/workspace/bustub/src/primer/hyperloglog.cpp","content":"#include \"primer/hyperloglog.h\"\n\n#include <algorithm>\n#include <cmath>\n#include <mutex>  // NOLINT\n\nnamespace bustub {\n\ntemplate <typename KeyType>\nHyperLogLog<KeyType>::HyperLogLog(int16_t n_bits) : cardinality_(0), n_bits_(n_bits) {\n  if (n_bits_ < 0) {\n    // Invalid: keep m_=0, registers_ empty, cardinality always 0\n    m_ = 0;\n  } else {\n    m_ = static_cast<int64_t>(1) << n_bits_;\n    registers_.resize(m_, 0);\n  }\n}\n\ntemplate <typename KeyType>\nauto HyperLogLog<KeyType>::ComputeBinary(const hash_t &hash) const -> std::bitset<BITSET_CAPACITY> {\n  return std::bitset<BITSET_CAPACITY>(hash);\n}\n\ntemplate <typename KeyType>\nauto HyperLogLog<KeyType>::PositionOfLeftmostOne(const std::bitset<BITSET_CAPACITY> &bset) const -> uint64_t {\n  // Count leading zeros from bit 63 (MSB) downward, return count + 1\n  // For all-zero bitset: return 65\n  for (int i = BITSET_CAPACITY - 1; i >= 0; i--) {\n    if (bset[i]) {\n      // Leading zeros = (BITSET_CAPACITY - 1 - i)\n      return static_cast<uint64_t>(BITSET_CAPACITY - 1 - i) + 1;\n    }\n  }\n  // All zeros: position is 65\n  return BITSET_CAPACITY + 1;\n}\n\ntemplate <typename KeyType>\nauto HyperLogLog<KeyType>::AddElem(KeyType val) -> void {\n  if (n_bits_ < 0) return;\n\n  hash_t hash = CalculateHash(val);\n\n  // Determine register index j and value portion\n  uint64_t j = 0;\n  uint64_t value = hash;\n  if (n_bits_ > 0) {\n    j = hash >> (static_cast<uint64_t>(64) - static_cast<uint64_t>(n_bits_));\n    value = hash << static_cast<uint64_t>(n_bits_);\n  }\n  // For n_bits_==0: j=0, value=hash (no shift needed)\n\n  auto bset = ComputeBinary(value);\n  uint64_t pos = PositionOfLeftmostOne(bset);\n\n  std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);\n  registers_[j] = std::max(registers_[j], pos);\n}\n\ntemplate <typename KeyType>\nauto HyperLogLog<KeyType>::ComputeCardinality() -> void {\n  if (n_bits_ < 0 || m_ == 0) {\n    cardinality_ = 0;\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // E = CONSTANT * m^2 / sum(2^{-M[j]})\n  double sum = 0.0;\n  for (int64_t j = 0; j < m_; j++) {\n    sum += std::pow(2.0, -static_cast<double>(registers_[j]));\n  }\n\n  double estimate = CONSTANT * static_cast<double>(m_) * static_cast<double>(m_) / sum;\n  cardinality_ = static_cast<size_t>(std::floor(estimate));\n}\n\ntemplate class HyperLogLog<int64_t>;\ntemplate class HyperLogLog<std::string>;\n\n}  // namespace bustub\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1892,"cache_read_input_tokens":104632,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1892,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":2,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"903c74f3-234a-4e0a-8bd4-0e6069c7048c","uuid":"90da35ad-9877-46fd-a0f1-e078e0a716fc"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01TMDDD1HfhBVkdreLbVQMEa","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01LU4ZX8C1QZjw7xCQJWL9Ck","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/workspace/bustub/src/primer/hyperloglog_presto.cpp","content":"#include \"primer/hyperloglog_presto.h\"\n\n#include <algorithm>\n#include <cmath>\n\nnamespace bustub {\n\ntemplate <typename KeyType>\nHyperLogLogPresto<KeyType>::HyperLogLogPresto(int16_t n_leading_bits)\n    : cardinality_(0), n_bits_(n_leading_bits) {\n  if (n_bits_ < 0) {\n    m_ = 0;\n    // dense_bucket_ stays empty\n  } else {\n    m_ = static_cast<int64_t>(1) << n_bits_;\n    dense_bucket_.resize(m_);  // bitset<4> default-initialized to 0\n  }\n}\n\ntemplate <typename KeyType>\nauto HyperLogLogPresto<KeyType>::AddElem(KeyType val) -> void {\n  if (n_bits_ < 0 || m_ == 0) return;\n\n  hash_t hash = CalculateHash(val);\n\n  // Determine register index and value bits\n  uint64_t j = 0;\n  uint64_t value_bits = hash;\n  if (n_bits_ > 0) {\n    j = hash >> (static_cast<uint64_t>(64) - static_cast<uint64_t>(n_bits_));\n    // Value bits: bottom (64 - n_bits_) bits\n    uint64_t mask = (static_cast<uint64_t>(1) << (64 - n_bits_)) - 1;\n    value_bits = hash & mask;\n  }\n  // For n_bits_==0: j=0, value_bits=hash\n\n  // Count trailing zeros; for all-zero: cap at (64 - n_bits_)\n  uint64_t trailing_zeros;\n  if (value_bits == 0) {\n    trailing_zeros = static_cast<uint64_t>(64 - n_bits_);\n  } else {\n    trailing_zeros = static_cast<uint64_t>(__builtin_ctzll(value_bits));\n  }\n\n  // Get current register value from dense + overflow buckets\n  uint64_t current_val =\n      static_cast<uint64_t>(dense_bucket_[j].to_ulong()) |\n      (static_cast<uint64_t>(overflow_bucket_[j].to_ulong()) << DENSE_BUCKET_SIZE);\n\n  // Update if new value is greater\n  if (trailing_zeros > current_val) {\n    dense_bucket_[j] = std::bitset<DENSE_BUCKET_SIZE>(trailing_zeros & 0xF);\n    overflow_bucket_[j] = std::bitset<OVERFLOW_BUCKET_SIZE>(trailing_zeros >> DENSE_BUCKET_SIZE);\n  }\n}\n\ntemplate <typename KeyType>\nauto HyperLogLogPresto<KeyType>::ComputeCardinality() -> void {\n  if (n_bits_ < 0 || m_ == 0) {\n    cardinality_ = 0;\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // E = CONSTANT * m^2 / sum(2^{-M[j]})\n  double sum = 0.0;\n  for (int64_t j = 0; j < m_; j++) {\n    uint64_t reg_val =\n        static_cast<uint64_t>(dense_bucket_[j].to_ulong()) |\n        (static_cast<uint64_t>(overflow_bucket_[j].to_ulong()) << DENSE_BUCKET_SIZE);\n    sum += std::pow(2.0, -static_cast<double>(reg_val));\n  }\n\n  double estimate = CONSTANT * static_cast<double>(m_) * static_cast<double>(m_) / sum;\n  cardinality_ = static_cast<uint64_t>(std::floor(estimate));\n}\n\ntemplate class HyperLogLogPresto<int64_t>;\ntemplate class HyperLogLogPresto<std::string>;\n\n}  // namespace bustub\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1892,"cache_read_input_tokens":104632,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1892,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":2,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"903c74f3-234a-4e0a-8bd4-0e6069c7048c","uuid":"af0d15f8-1045-4a94-9cdf-d1b67823ffc9"}

i >= 0; i--) {\n    if (bset[i]) {\n      // Leading zeros = (BITSET_CAPACITY - 1 - i)\n      return static_cast<uint64_t>(BITSET_CAPACITY - 1 - i) + 1;\n    }\n  }\n  // All zeros: position is 65\n  return BITSET_CAPACITY + 1;\n}\n\ntemplate <typename KeyType>\nauto HyperLogLog<KeyType>::AddElem(KeyType val) -> void {\n  if (n_bits_ < 0) return;\n\n  hash_t hash = CalculateHash(val);\n\n  // Determine register index j and value portion\n  uint64_t j = 0;\n  uint64_t value = hash;\n  if (n_bits_ > 0) {\n    j = hash >> (static_cast<uint64_t>(64) - static_cast<uint64_t>(n_bits_));\n    value = hash << static_cast<uint64_t>(n_bits_);\n  }\n  // For n_bits_==0: j=0, value=hash (no shift needed)\n\n  auto bset = ComputeBinary(value);\n  uint64_t pos = PositionOfLeftmostOne(bset);\n\n  std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_);\n  registers_[j] = std::max(registers_[j], pos);\n}\n\ntemplate <typename KeyType>\nauto HyperLogLog<KeyType>::ComputeCardinality() -> void {\n  if (n_bits_ < 0 || m_ == 0) {\n    cardinality_ = 0;\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // E = CONSTANT * m^2 / sum(2^{-M[j]})\n  double sum = 0.0;\n  for (int64_t j = 0; j < m_; j++) {\n    sum += std::pow(2.0, -static_cast<double>(registers_[j]));\n  }\n\n  double estimate = CONSTANT * static_cast<double>(m_) * static_cast<double>(m_) / sum;\n  cardinality_ = static_cast<size_t>(std::floor(estimate));\n}\n\ntemplate class HyperLogLog<int64_t>;\ntemplate class HyperLogLog<std::string>;\n\n}  // namespace bustub\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}
